title: Manage and validate deterministic rules
preconditions: The rule references only allowed canonical fields and an active category identifier; a private representative evaluation corpus and current normalization version are available for activation decisions.
postconditions: The active rule set is immutable and version-addressable; superseded versions remain inspectable; activation evidence and input fingerprint are attributable; no transaction assignment occurs.
main_flow: CLASSIFY saves a draft rule version with owner attribution and rationale; validates its bounded predicate vocabulary; evaluates the candidate rule set against representative and held-out evidence; reports coverage, no-suggestion, conflict, drift, and incorrect-application canaries; and activates or retires a version only through an explicit owner command.
alt_flows: Reject unsupported or semantically unsafe fields and predicates.
Reject references to unknown or archived categories.
Refuse activation when required private evidence is absent, stale, or contains an incorrect-application canary.
Surface overlapping incompatible matches as conflicts instead of silently choosing a winner.
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner creates, changes, retires, or proposes activation of a classification rule.","status":"draft"}
